title: Reson and Sensuallyte
summary: The pinned General Corpus XML is a bounded navigational enrichment of the raw 2006 U-M encoding, which derives from a complete 380-TIFF capture of Ernst Sieper's 1901-1903 EETS edition. Sieper transcribes Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Fairfax 16, ff. 202r-300r, as principal text and collates John Stow's 1558 Add. MS 29729 copy. The one unfinished 7,042-line Middle English work freely translates and expands French lines 1-4,873 of the anonymous Echecs amoureux en vers; the physical French exemplar is unknown, so the three surviving French witnesses remain supporting rather than primary sources.
review_notes: The final graph has 21 entities, 27 relations, 17 access records, 22 separately scoped rights records, 3 editorial-practice records, 21 evidence records, and 6 open questions.

The direct digital/documentary path is repository XML -> raw U-M encoding -> U-M TIFFs -> Sieper edition -> Fairfax 16; the separate intellectual relation is whole English work version_of French lines 1-4873.

Complete manuscript objects were cached where possible. Fairfax 16 is present at ff. 202r-300r; the complete SLUB and Harvard French-source objects explicitly record the Middle English target as absent.
The Marciana full-facsimile endpoint returned HTTP 503 on 2026-07-17. This dated access limitation is not modeled as loss or permanent withdrawal.
Rights, licenses, and provider terms remain attached to the exact historical text, edition, encoding, catalog, physical carrier, or reproduction layer supported by evidence.

entities.label: Ernst Sieper, Lydgate's Reson and Sensuallyte, EETS Extra Series 84 and 89 (1901-1903)
entities.description: Part I supplies the manuscript descriptions, edited text, Furnivall side-notes, and glossary; Part II supplies studies and notes. The Part II preface is dated Munich, May 1904, but its title page is 1903.

date_statements.label: Publication
date_statements.notes: Part II bears 1903 on its title page; its preface is signed Munich, May 1904.

bibliographic.citation: Ernst Sieper, ed., Lydgate's Reson and Sensuallyte, EETS Extra Series 84 and 89 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trubner & Co., 1901-1903).
bibliographic.title: Lydgate's Reson and Sensuallyte
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series

entities.label: Reson and Sensuallyte (abstract Middle English work)
entities.description: One unfinished 7,042-line Middle English verse translation/adaptation, cautiously attributed to John Lydgate.
notes: The catalog_description type is the schema representation of the abstract work, not a modern catalog record.
John Lydgate remains an attributed translator-adapter, not an unqualified author.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Fairfax 16, ff. 202r-300r
entities.description: Principal witness used by Sieper; a mid-fifteenth-century English parchment codex. The exact work starts on f. 202r and ends on f. 300r.

date_statements.label: Production
date_statements.value: likely written in the 1440s, possibly completed in 1450

notes: Reson and Sensuallyte occupies ff. 202r-300r.
The first XML folio milestone is 202v after 39 poem lines, but direct image inspection proves that the work begins on f. 202r.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF facsimile of MS Fairfax 16
entities.description: Complete 721-canvas object; target canvas inventory positions 417-613 inclusive.

entities.label: Google/Harvard page-image scan of the complete Sieper EETS set
entities.description: Complete 400-page PDF derivative distributed by Internet Archive.

entities.label: London, British Library, Add. MS 29729, ff. 184r-286v
entities.description: John Stow's 1558 imperfect copy, collated by Sieper. DIMEV's shared ff. 184-186v locator is retained only as an identified catalog-data error.
notes: DIMEV gives ff. 184-186v; the official catalog and Sieper establish ff. 184r-286v, so the DIMEV endpoint is retained only as a documented data error.
The work-specific copied_from relation to Fairfax comes from Sieper and is not generalized from the British Library collection-level Shirley statement.

entities.label: Echecs amoureux en vers (anonymous French verse work)
entities.description: Anonymous, unfinished Middle French dream-vision allegory of about 30,000 octosyllabic couplet lines, dated 1370-1380 by JONAS. It is distinct from the prose moralized commentary associated with Evrart de Conty.
notes: The catalog_description type represents the abstract French work; it is distinct from the prose moralized commentary associated with Evrart de Conty.

entities.label: Dresden, SLUB, Mscr.Dresd.Oc.66
entities.description: Complete surviving French witness. The analogous first 4,873 source lines run from f. 1r through f. 25r, IIIF canvases PHYS_0006-PHYS_0054.

date_statements.label: Text copying
date_statements.value: last third of the fourteenth century
date_statements.notes: JONAS separately dates completion of the illumination to 1482-1502; generic provider metadata giving 1450 is not substituted for this layered chronology.

entities.label: SLUB complete digital facsimile of Mscr.Dresd.Oc.66
entities.description: Complete provider PDF and 301-canvas IIIF object.

entities.label: Cambridge, Houghton Library, MS Fr 278
entities.description: Two-leaf French fragment identified with Echecs amoureux by JONAS/Biblissima but cataloged by Harvard as an unidentified advice poem; exact verse range unresolved.

notes: The current complete digital object has six canvases.
JONAS and Biblissima identify the fragment with Echecs amoureux; Harvard describes it as an unidentified advice poem. The exact verse range remains unresolved.

entities.label: Harvard complete IIIF facsimile of MS Fr 278
entities.description: All six canvases representing both surviving leaves and associated surfaces.

entities.label: Venice, Biblioteca Nazionale Marciana, Str. App. 23 (=267), ff. 37-202
entities.description: Late-fourteenth-century French witness, acephalous for lines 1-3,028. Its overlap with the English source segment is French lines 3,029-4,873, approximately ff. 37r-58r.

entities.label: Internet Culturale full facsimile route for Marciana Str. App. 23 (=267)
entities.description: JONAS records a full facsimile route. The endpoint returned HTTP 503 on 2026-07-17; no local complete copy was obtained.
notes: The checked HTTP 503 response is a dated access limitation, not evidence of loss or permanent withdrawal.

entities.description: Current 131-page scholarly transcription used only as supporting evidence and not inferred to have an open redistribution license.

bibliographic.title: Les esches amoureux

rights.basis: Medieval historical text; no claim is extended to modern editions, markup, catalog prose, or photographs.
Fourteenth-century historical text; no claim is extended to current scholarly editions or photographs.
Publication dates 1901 and 1903. Provider requests attached to particular scans remain separate.
Current item page says the materials are public domain and supplies No Copyright - United States.

The current U-M item page says the delivered materials are public domain and supplies No Copyright - United States. This record is attached directly to the page-image facsimile rather than inherited from the encoding.
The raw header says that where applicable copyright and other distribution restrictions may apply. This generic older warning is not merged with the current item-level statement.
Pinned repository LICENSE is the Unlicense but repeatedly describes software; do not use it alone for a broad XML-content rights claim.

The file labels the scanned book public domain but requests personal noncommercial file use, retention of the Google watermark/attribution, no automated querying, and legal compliance. These reproduction-file requests are distinct from the edition's copyright status.
Versioned DIMEV release license; it does not license manuscript images.
Official IIIF attribution; commercial reuse is outside the stated license absent separate permission.
Official catalog terms; does not license the manuscript or ordered images.
Current Imaging Services terms require written permission before copying or reproduction of supplied material.
Provider IIIF/METS rights metadata.
Harvard allows any-purpose use with citation under its public-domain digital-reproductions policy; Houghton asks for notification, not permission, for a facsimile edition or substantial publication.
Current portal terms; commercial users are directed to the owning institution. No images are local.
Treat as research evidence only; no open redistribution license was established.
